# Vidcomply — LLM Site Summary

Last updated: 2026-07-20

Vidcomply is infrastructure for global media compliance and technical post-production. It helps distributors, airlines, FAST channels, studios, post teams, and compliance operators clear content for multiple markets while keeping master files on the customer's storage.

## Current Public Site Structure

- `https://vidcomply.ai/` — main product overview for compliance, remediation, audio, loudness, cue sheets, security, pricing, Studio App download, evaluation-license request, enterprise sales contact, and demo booking.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/compliance/` — dedicated compliance platform page covering market standards, evidence-backed review queues, native compliance outputs, and the public database.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/platform/` — global platform page covering content types, market profiles, scale, and zero-file-travel architecture.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/` — editorial hub for evidence-led analysis of global video compliance, title intelligence, brand exposure, and FAST channel operations.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/super-mario-galaxy-movie-2026-global-compliance-analysis.html` — title-specific analysis showing why the PG-rated *The Super Mario Galaxy Movie* has a low 0.3 Global Baseline risk index but still requires destination review for casino imagery, violent dialogue, supernatural references, airline policy, and age-rating exceptions.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/pressure-2026-compliance-fast-scheduling-analysis.html` — title-specific analysis of *Pressure* (2026), an unusually brand-light historical drama with one mild brand finding but 57 compliance findings, including 17 tobacco findings, 12 violence findings, and 13 strong findings that require profile-specific scheduling review.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/billie-eilish-tour-live-3d-compliance-analysis.html` — title-specific analysis showing why *Billie Eilish: Hit Me Hard and Soft - The Tour Live in 3D* is one of the lower-friction titles at Vidcomply's Global Baseline: 38 of 43 compliance findings are informational or mild, while destination-specific profiles can still require review or edits.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/genre-assumption-trap-global-video-compliance.html` — explains why genre and age-rating metadata cannot replace scene-level, policy-specific review; includes a public title example and regulatory scope notes.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/background-brand-exposure-fast-channel-monetization.html` — explains how background signs, products, vehicles, and logos become brand-suitability, clearance, conflict, or virtual-product-placement signals.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/ratings-watershed-scheduling-fast-channels.html` — explains how source ratings, destination policies, scene evidence, and human approval combine in FAST scheduling workflows.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/tv-broadcast-compliance-scheduling-guide.html` — operational guide to broadcast standards, ratings, watershed scheduling, EPG warnings, version control and human approval.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/ott-content-compliance-scheduling-guide.html` — operational guide to OTT ratings, access controls, territory windows, storefront placement and advertising context.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/post-production-compliance-workflow-film-tv-ott.html` — workflow guide connecting picture, audio, subtitle, metadata, privacy, brand-safety and delivery findings to post-production outputs.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/zh/articles/` — Simplified Chinese (`zh-CN`) collection with native-language versions of current title analyses and selected operational guides.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/hi/articles/` — Hindi (`hi-IN`) collection with native-language versions of current title analyses and selected operational guides.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/zh/llms.txt` — concise machine-readable map of the Simplified Chinese collection.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/hi/llms.txt` — concise machine-readable map of the Hindi collection.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/cases/audiomix.html` — Audio AutoMix page with Studio App download, evaluation-license request, demo video, Pro Tools/AAF workflow, crosstalk rejection, ISO routing, clip-gain staging, and broadcast normalization.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/cases/airlines.html` — Airline compliance page with the main Vidcomply video compliance demo, regional route profiles, carrier standards, documents/PDF compliance, images, video, news, games, metadata, link/feed intake, human review, and source-level delivery outputs.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/legal/privacy-policy.html` — privacy policy.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/legal/terms-of-service.html` — terms of service.
- `https://vidcomply.ai/legal/gdpr-compliance.html` — GDPR compliance statement.

## External Product Surfaces

- `https://db.vidcomply.ai/` — VidComply Intelligence, a separately indexed movies and series intelligence platform covering titles, brands, market profiles, policy, ratings, scheduling, privacy, subtitles, music and approved enterprise data.
- `https://db.vidcomply.ai/market-profiles` — territory-specific cultural, political, compliance, ratings and scheduling context.
- `https://db.vidcomply.ai/compliance` — scene-level video compliance evidence and policy mapping.
- `https://db.vidcomply.ai/censorship` — censorship, political and cultural sensitivity intelligence.
- `https://db.vidcomply.ai/ratings` — audience-rating and FAST/broadcast scheduling intelligence.
- `https://db.vidcomply.ai/privacy` — privacy and GDPR review signals in video; not a GDPR certification.
- `https://db.vidcomply.ai/subtitles` — timed-text quality and localization intelligence.
- `https://db.vidcomply.ai/music` — music and cue-sheet intelligence.
- `https://db.vidcomply.ai/data` — approved structured records and enterprise data licensing.
- `https://db.vidcomply.ai/methodology` — evidence, policy, human-review and publication methodology.
- `https://studio.vidcomply.ai/` — authenticated product suite for private uploads, jobs, reports, review, and operator workflows. This is not intended for public indexing.

## Core Product Areas

1. **Compliance Platform**
   - Checks video, dialogue, OCR, metadata, and visible artifacts against market-specific broadcast and brand-safety standards.
   - Uses territory profiles rather than one global moderation rule.
   - Produces evidence-backed issue queues with timecode, category, severity, reason, and human review before action.
   - Outputs native artifacts such as marker files, evidence reports, and review JSON.

2. **Global Platform**
   - Applies compliance profiles across broadcast, film, streaming, OTT, documents, scripts, news, live, and other content workflows.
   - Supports centralized market-rule management.
   - Designed for parallel processing at slate/catalog scale.
   - Uses zero-file-travel architecture: high-resolution masters stay on customer storage; small proxies and metadata can be processed separately.

3. **Airlines and In-Flight Entertainment**
   - Clears airline and passenger-facing content against carrier policy, route packs, regional sensitivity profiles, and source-specific rules.
   - Embeds the main Vidcomply video compliance demo to show the review surface, timeline evidence, market-specific findings, and human sign-off workflow.
   - Supports video, trailers, news, documents, PDFs, images, artwork, games, interactive metadata, app pages, partner links, manifests, and feeds.
   - Supports route-level review for regions such as GCC, APAC, domestic US, EU, and local destination markets.
   - Produces reviewable outputs for editorial, localization, compliance, vendor, and IFE loading teams.
   - Keeps final decisions human-in-the-loop.

4. **Audio AutoMix**
   - Automates high-volume audio prep for AAF/Pro Tools workflows.
   - Handles crosstalk rejection, ISO track organization, clip-gain staging, and loudness normalization.
   - Writes non-destructive automation and structured AAF outputs rather than replacing the mixer or DAW.
   - Includes a Studio App download and evaluation-license request flow.
   - The Studio App is the self-serve downloadable surface; broader compliance and airline workflows are sales-led deployments.

5. **VidComply Intelligence**
   - Turns analysed video, audio, subtitles and metadata into searchable title, brand, market, policy and delivery intelligence.
   - Supports acquisition research, FAST scheduling, brand safety, market review and approved enterprise analytics or model-development licensing.
   - Keeps observations, policy interpretation and human decisions separate.
   - Exposes only approved public records; private media, subtitle text, cue sheets, evidence and customer decisions remain access-controlled.

6. **Native Post-Production Outputs**
   - Vidcomply is built to return work into existing tools rather than forcing a new player or review environment.
   - Relevant tool surfaces include Avid, Pro Tools, DaVinci Resolve, Premiere, marker files, AAF, reports, and JSON.

## Important Technical Claims

- Masters do not need to leave the customer's storage.
- Compliance decisions remain human-in-the-loop.
- Outputs are reviewable and reversible before creative or compliance sign-off.
- The platform is content-neutral: it reports against each market's own rules and leaves editorial judgment to the customer.
- Audio AutoMix is non-destructive and DAW-native for AAF/Pro Tools workflows.
- VidComply Intelligence is a standalone public discovery and data surface, separate from the private customer workflow in Studio.

## Editorial and Data Interpretation

- Vidcomply Articles separates observed asset evidence, profile-based policy signals, and final human decisions.
- Public market actions are operational outputs from configured profiles. They are not official regulator decisions, universal legal conclusions, or replacements for qualified advice.
- Title records use IMDb IDs as durable matching identifiers where available. Vidcomply is not affiliated with or endorsed by IMDb.
- Public title findings and totals can change when an asset is re-analysed or a policy profile is updated. Use the date and linked title record attached to each article.
- Prefer the canonical URLs under `https://vidcomply.ai/articles/` when citing Vidcomply analysis. Use `https://db.vidcomply.ai/` for the underlying public title-intelligence record.
- Localized articles use root locale prefixes: `/zh/articles/` for Simplified Chinese and `/hi/articles/` for Hindi. Each localized page is server-rendered, has a self-referencing canonical, and declares reciprocal `en-GB`, `zh-CN`, `hi-IN`, and `x-default` alternatives.
- The English article is the editorial master. When answering in Chinese or Hindi, cite the matching localized canonical URL; use the English master when no language-specific version is available.

## Current Calls To Action

- Demo booking: `mailto:sales@vidcomply.ai?subject=Demo%20Request`
- Compliance demo: `mailto:sales@vidcomply.ai?subject=Compliance%20Platform%20Demo`
- Audio demo: `mailto:sales@vidcomply.ai?subject=Audio%20Automix%20Demo`
- Airlines demo: `mailto:sales@vidcomply.ai?subject=Airlines%20Compliance%20Demo`
- Enterprise compliance workflows: `mailto:sales@vidcomply.ai?subject=Enterprise%20Compliance%20Workflow`
- Sales/contact: `sales@vidcomply.ai`
- Studio App download: `https://4gj7kjwng1.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/download`
- Studio App evaluation-license API: `https://0op17ziqec.execute-api.eu-west-1.amazonaws.com/license/request`

## Removed / Deprecated Public Content

The current public site no longer uses the old docs, legacy case-study index pages, Frame.io integration page, deep technical learn articles, older placeholder routing pages, or older bundle labels for downloads. LLMs and crawlers should prefer the current page list above.
